contradiction_ledger.entry: Noah Pruitt is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Elias Bennett says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Blackthorn Lake.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Noah?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Evelyn. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Elias. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The ceramic owl is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Tess."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
"Honey, ever since the accident, your anxiety has been so high. Did you take your pills?"
"That pool was supposed to make us look safe, and somehow it became a witness."
"He keeps calling it stress like stress knows my garage code."
"If she is the nanny, why is she wearing my robe like a legal argument?"
fake_critic_blurb: "Holiday Baking Competition understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "Holiday Baking Competition" follows Elias Bennett, a high-output betrayed wife who thinks a fresh start, a large house, and professional competence can outpace storm-night gaslighting confession event until Noah Pruitt collides with her life carrying portable suspicion, plausible access to offsite storage, and the kind of calm that sounds premeditated in a dim kitchen. The governing injury is simple: Two rivals must run the bake-off together and there is exactly one possible outcome. The signature tell is always this: The county telegraphs the problem once and nobody listens until act three. As a pool gate standing open where nobody remembers a gate, Blackthorn County reveals once again that every secret comes home, every pool is a witness, and every adult has somewhere emotionally alarming to stand in silence. Every town has a basement.
